Its economy policy says work could include litter-picking, graffiti removal and drain clearing, and that anyone who refuses or slacks on the job would be denied benefits.

topic: Repeal net zero and prioritise energy security
title: Repeal net zero and prioritise energy security
summary: Restore Britain says energy should be cheap, reliable and scalable. It would repeal net zero goals by abolishing the Climate Change Act 2008, prioritise fossil-fuel investment where needed, pursue a Britain First energy-security strategy, prevent foreign ownership of energy infrastructure, lift the ban on North Sea oil and gas exploration, and re-examine shale gas.

title: Automate the Tube and prioritise domestic infrastructure

topic: Election integrity and restricted postal voting
title: Election integrity and restricted postal voting
summary: Restore Britain says it would safeguard election integrity by abolishing postal voting except for people with genuine, proven need, launching a full review of past irregularities with prosecutions for wrongdoing, deporting foreign or dual nationals found guilty, and barring foreign nationals from voting or standing in British local, regional or national elections.

Prosecute people found to have manipulated ballots, intimidated voters or exploited vulnerable communities.
Deport foreign or dual nationals found guilty of election wrongdoing.
Prevent foreign nationals from voting or standing in British local, regional or national elections.

topic: British interests first foreign policy
title: British interests first foreign policy
summary: Restore Britain says foreign policy should put British interests first over international institutions or foreign states. It says military aid and armed-force deployments would be judged by the interests of the British people, foreign aid that does not advance Britain's national interests would be scrapped, Britain should rebuild and rearm, and DEI should be abolished across government departments including the armed forces.
